command: Invoke-Command -ScriptBlock { $target='10.0.0.25'; $seqB64='NzAwMCw4MDAwLDkwMDA='; $ports = [Text.Encoding]::ASCII.GetString([Convert]::FromBase64String($seqB64)).Split(',') | ForEach-Object { [int]$_ }; foreach($p in $ports){ $null = Test-NetConnection -ComputerName $target -Port $p -InformationLevel Quiet; Start-Sleep -Milliseconds 300 } }
explanation: Sends a base64-encoded port-knock sequence (7000, 8000, 9000) using TCP SYN probes to a target. This can unlock a hidden service on the remote if it runs a knock responder. Detection: alert on repeated failed connection attempts to uncommon high ports in rapid succession from the same source; match known knocking sequences. No elevation required; UAC not triggered.
rationale_steps: Decode knock sequence from base64 and iterate ports in order.
Send TCP SYN via Test-NetConnection to emulate a knock sequence.
Rely on remote knock daemon to enable functionality upon correct sequence.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: PowerShell command is well-formed with balanced braces/quotes, valid parameters, and proper separators.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: Implements a plausible port-knock via Test-NetConnection aligning with T1205, but the use of [Text.Encoding] (instead of [System.Text.Encoding]) would likely cause a runtime type resolution error, preventing execution as written.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The PowerShell command is perfectly well-formed with balanced braces, proper string handling, valid Base64 decoding pipeline, correct array splitting, integer casting, and proper loop/sleep syntax.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command accurately implements a port-knocking sequence (T1205) by decoding a Base64-encoded port list and sequentially probing ports 7000, 8000, 9000 via TCP SYN using Test-NetConnection with timed intervals, which is a realistic and operationally plausible method to trigger a knock daemon on a remote host.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The PowerShell command is perfectly well-formed with correct variable assignments, balanced braces, valid .NET class calls, and proper cmdlet usage.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command effectively implements a port-knocking sequence by decoding a base64 string and iterating through ports using Test-NetConnection, accurately representing the T1205 technique.
